Tests that `String.prototype.charCodeAt` returns NaN for an out-of-bounds index.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses an assertion to verify the behavior of the `String.prototype.charCodeAt` method in JavaScript. It checks that calling `charCodeAt(3)` on the string `"abc"` returns `NaN`, as the index `3` is out of bounds for the string `"abc"`, which has a length of 3. The `assert` function is used to ensure that the result of `isNaN` is true, confirming that the method behaves as expected when accessing a character index that does not exist in the string. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of string handling in JavaScript.
